---
title: "nitrix.sparse.ell_sectioned"
generated: true
signature-hash: 4431e52d2edac054
description: "Sectioned ELL -- bucketed-row format for variable-degree adjacencies."
listing:
  id: nitrix-sparse-ell_sectioned-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-sparse-ell_sectioned/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.sparse.ell_sectioned`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Sectioned ELL -- bucketed-row format for variable-degree adjacencies.

The naive ELL layout pads every row to the global $k_{\max}$.  That is
memory-efficient when $k_{\max} \approx \operatorname{median}(k)$ but
pathological when the worst-case row is much larger -- e.g. distance-thresholded
neighbourhoods in irregular point clouds ($k_{\max}$ can be 10-100x the
median) or atlas-parcel adjacencies (parcel sizes vary by 1-2 orders of
magnitude).

The sectioned-ELL trick: bucket rows by $\lceil \log_2(\mathrm{degree})
\rceil$, run [`semiring_ell_matmul`](nitrix-semiring.qmd) once per bucket with
the bucket's *local* $k_{\max}$, and scatter the results back to the
original row order.  Memory cost is dominated by the largest bucket, not by the
worst-case row.

This is a Python-level wrapper around the existing
[`semiring_ell_matmul`](nitrix-semiring.qmd) kernel -- no new kernel code.
Construction is *not* JIT-compatible because the per-bucket shapes are
data-dependent.  Reuse is JIT-friendly (the per-bucket matmuls trace once and
the dispatch is a static Python loop).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_cols` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `ncol` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `n_rows` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-sparse-ell_sectioned-symbols}
:::
